LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RON BOCHNER MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. THE POLICY BEHIND THE MANDATORY FEE ARBITRATION STATUTES, TO ALLEVIATE THE DISPARITY IN BARGAINING POWER IN ATTORNEY FEE MATTERS, WOULD NOT BE SERVED BY DISMISSAL. DEFENDANT AS AN ATTORNEY WAS SOPHISTICATED AND KNEW OF HIS RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice And Motion To Dismiss Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RON BOCHNER Motion to Dismiss Complaint. Scott Kalkin, Esq. of Roboostoff & Kalkin (415) 732-0282 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Ron Bochner, Defendant in Pro Per Appearing thru CourtCall and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Dismiss is denied. The policy behind the mandatory fee arbitration statutes, to alleviate the disparity in bargaining power in attorney fee matters, would not be served by dismissal. Defendant as an attorney was sophisticated and knew of his right to arbitrate. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
